- **P0/P1/P2: none.** The descriptor-only secondary-workflow service slice is correctly implemented, tested, and validated; no-write/no-real-data and read-only/non-final-Claude boundaries all hold; the pack does not over-claim (production_ready/runtime_ready false, human approval required for runtime). All 18 ladder commands recorded exit 0, and the contract projects CP00-761 as latest with `validation.valid: true`.
- **Two P3 informational notes** (non-blocking): cosmetic Hermes packet-ID doubling in `service.js:152`, and a minor classification nuance for regenerated plan/queue files in `changed-file-scope.json` — neither affects closeout.

Independent read-only inspection of registry.js, service.js, validators.js, index.js, import-plan.js, mappers.js, test/model.test.js, the regenerated contracts/migration-platform-contract.json, and the recorded command ladder confirms the pack-level claim (production_ready_flag=migration_platform_secondary_service_descriptor_verified) is supported. The CP761 pack binding, requirements (required_section_rows RP25.P02.M04 = SERVICE_ROWS.slice(10,20), matching the 10 manifest titles and the ui:2/implementation:3/claude_review:1/failure_recovery:2/test:2 distribution), case set, Hermes evidence packet, Claude review packet, and closeout handoff are present and internally consistent. model.test.js adds CP00-761 binding/coverage, runtime-closed descriptor, and contract-latest tests; the contract projects CP00-761 as latest_pack/latest_projection with validation.valid=true. All 18 recorded commands (node --check on every source file, closeout-pack:validate, rp25:migration-platform:validate, node --test, npm test, npm run validate, spec/weighted/fullplan/goal/rp00 validators, git diff --check, npm run build) returned exit_code 0. No-write/no-real-data boundaries hold: MIGRATION_PLATFORM_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION plus per-row descriptor_only/runtime-closed guards, import-plan and mapper descriptors runtime-closed, and no secrets or real client/matter data (grep clean). The pack correctly does NOT over-claim: manifest production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor; descriptor authority_boundary sets claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true (runtime_opening_pack_id CP00-762). Hermes patterns are used only as deterministic descriptor/evidence metadata (emits_runtime_receipt=false, writes_product_state=false), not product runtime. The pending adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, and claude-review-result.json are correct placeholders that explicitly avoid claiming a passing review and are not counted as evidence. Claude remains a read-only, non-final reviewer; the read-only tool boundary and CP178+ hardening blockers (auth pass, non-empty valid JSON, required fields) are satisfied. Two non-blocking P3 informational observations are noted; no P0/P1/P2 issues. Verdict: PASS.","findings":[{"id":"CP761-P3-01","severity":"P3","title":"Hermes evidence_packet identifier embeds the full descriptor name (cosmetic)","file":"packages/migration/src/service.js","line":152,"details":"In createDescriptor(), descriptor.hermes_packet.evidence_packet renders as "H25.CP00-761.migration_platform_MigrationPlatformCp761SecondaryServiceDescriptor", duplicating the MigrationPlatform... token, whereas the standalone createMigrationPlatformCp761SecondaryServiceHermesEvidencePacket() uses the cleaner id "CP00-761.H25.migration_platform_secondary_service". Two non-conflicting ID schemes coexist. Both are deterministic and the value is descriptor metadata only (emits_runtime_receipt=false). The pattern is identical across CP756-CP761, so it reflects accepted precedent rather than a regression.","recommendation":"Optional future cleanup to normalize the two Hermes packet ID schemes; no action required for CP00-761 closeout.","post_review_disposition":"Acknowledged as informational/cosmetic; does not block pack closeout."},{"id":"CP761-P3-02","severity":"P3","title":"Regenerated plan/queue files classified as unrelated in changed-file-scope.json","file":"artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-761/changed-file-scope.json","line":208,"details":"unrelated_dirty_files_preserved lists docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json and next-pack-queue.json, but command-evidence.json shows `npm run closeout-pack-plan:generate` ran in this pack's ladder and the plan's live_state now points to CP00-761 (generated_at 2026-06-15T13:55:38Z), so these files are regenerated as a side effect of closing CP00-761. The manifest's worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved omits them.
